fact: When bundling Pi extensions with tsup, @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, @mariozechner/pi-ai, @mariozechner/pi-tui, and typebox must NOT be bundled into the output. They must be declared in peerDependencies with "*" range so Pi's runtime provides them. The tsup config should use external: ['@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent'] and NOT include them in noExternal.

Bundling @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent inside a Pi extension tsup bundle causes jiti internals (used by Pi for TS loading) to be duplicated in the bundle. Jiti's scope renames __dirname in its injected code, which can cause __dirname to resolve to undefined when Pi loads the bundle. This produces the cryptic error: 'The "path" argument must be of type string or an instance of URL. Received undefined' from any path.join(__dirname, ...) call in the extension.

Inside Pi extensions, __dirname should be avoided when possible because Pi uses jiti for extension loading. When bundled with tsup, __dirname may resolve unpredictably or be undefined depending on jiti's wrapper scope. Use process.cwd() for runtime-relative paths, and for package-relative assets (like config.json), resolve them differently or bundle the config as inline JSON instead of reading from __dirname.

For Pi extension npm packages that bundle internal dependencies (like pi-agent-bus-node), bundledDependencies must be declared in package.json alongside dependencies. Tsup should only bundle YOUR packages (noExternal: ['pi-agent-bus-node']) while keeping Pi core packages external. The package tarball must include the bundled dep files so they resolve at Pi install time.

The fix for the 'path argument must be string, received undefined' Pi extension load error is threefold: (1) Move @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent from devDependencies to peerDependencies with "*" range in package.json, (2) In tsup.config.ts, add external: ['@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent', 'typebox'] and remove @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent from noExternal, (3) Replace __dirname references in index.ts with process.cwd() or inline config defaults.

The Pi extension error 'The "path" argument must be of type string or an instance of URL. Received undefined' is a SYMPTOM of bundling @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent (or jiti) into a tsup bundle. When Pi loads the extension, the bundled jiti code renames the __dirname variable in its scope, causing all path.join(__dirname, ...) calls to receive undefined. The fix is always to externalize Pi core deps in tsup.config.ts and declare them as peerDependencies in package.json.

Do NOT bundle Pi extensions with tools like tsup. Pi uses jiti to load and execute raw TypeScript files directly. Third-party dependencies belong in dependencies and will be installed by Pi, while core Pi dependencies belong in peerDependencies and must never be bundled. Distribute raw .ts files directly.
